edges.description: Demyelination disrupts peripheral motor and sensory signaling.
Progressive demyelination over weeks produces accumulating weakness and sensory dysfunction.
Barrier dysfunction allows protein elevation in CSF without a specific pathogen.
edges.source: Blood-nerve barrier dysfunction with elevated CSF protein

edges.description: Inflammatory cell infiltration can drive peripheral nerve myelin injury.
edges.source: Immune cell infiltration and macrophage-mediated demyelination

edges.description: CSF inflammatory biomarkers may reflect neuroinflammatory barrier dysfunction.
edges.target: Blood-nerve barrier dysfunction with elevated CSF protein

nodes.description: Elevated CSF protein is common in SIDP and supports blood-nerve or blood-CSF barrier dysfunction in inflammatory demyelinating neuropathy.
nodes.id: Blood-nerve barrier dysfunction with elevated CSF protein

evidence.snippet: Spinal fluid protein was high in 93% of cases.

nodes.description: IL-8 is an emerging CSF biomarker in adjacent inflammatory demyelinating neuropathies, but current evidence is not SIDP-specific.

evidence.reference_title: Validation of elevated levels of interleukin-8 in the cerebrospinal fluid, and discovery of new biomarkers in patients with GBS and CIDP using a proximity extension assay.
evidence.snippet: IL8 in CSF is validated as a diagnostic biomarker in GBS and CIDP

nodes.description: Prednisone was the main therapy in the foundational SIDP cohort, with high rates of complete or partial recovery.

evidence.snippet: Almost all patients were treated with prednisone and some with additional immunotherapies. Complete recovery was achieved in 69% of cases and partial recovery in others.

nodes.description: Nerve biopsy evidence of inflammatory cells supports immune-cell infiltration as part of the acquired demyelinating neuropathy process; macrophage-mediated myelin injury is a plausible effector mechanism in the CIDP-spectrum context.
nodes.id: Immune cell infiltration and macrophage-mediated demyelination

evidence.snippet: Supportive criteria included high spinal fluid protein level (>55 mg/dL) and inflammatory cells in the nerve biopsy.

nodes.description: SIDP is characterized by subacute immune-mediated demyelination in multiple peripheral nerves, causing slowed conduction, conduction block, weakness, sensory loss, and reduced reflexes.

evidence.snippet: electrophysiologic evidence of demyelination in at least two nerves

nodes.description: IVIg is used in CIDP-spectrum inflammatory demyelinating neuropathies and may be considered when SIDP versus CIDP remains uncertain.

evidence.reference_title: European Academy of Neurology/Peripheral Nerve Society guideline on diagnosis and treatment of chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculoneuropathy: Report of a joint Task Force-Second revision.
evidence.snippet: intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIg) or corticosteroids are strongly recommended as initial treatment in typical CIDP and CIDP variants

nodes.description: Progressive motor dysfunction and weakness are common SIDP manifestations.

evidence.snippet: The two most common neuropathy types were a symmetric motor-sensory neuropathy and a pure motor neuropathy.

nodes.description: SIDP presents as neuropathy affecting more than one limb.

evidence.snippet: progressive motor and/or sensory dysfunction consistent with neuropathy in more than one limb with time to nadir between 4 and 8 weeks

nodes.description: The defining temporal mechanism is progression over four to eight weeks with no relapse during adequate follow-up in definite SIDP.

evidence.snippet: progressive motor and/or sensory dysfunction consistent with neuropathy in more than one limb with time to nadir between 4 and 8 weeks
